title: stylidium rubricalyx
intro: Stylidium rubricalyx, also known as the Trigger Plant, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Stylidiaceae. This plant is native to Western Australia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The medicinal properties of Stylidium rubricalyx are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of Stylidium rubricalyx include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions and provide overall wellness benefits.
